record.text: The biggest leap in my output quality came from one switch: stop writing prompts like requests and start writing them as systems. Role + context + output format + constraints + examples. Most people blame the model when the real issue is vague instructions dressed up as a prompt.
This solves the thing nobody talks about: runaway token burn from a stuck agent. Bookmarking. Would love a version that triggers a webhook when context window crosses 80%.

Curious what the verdict was for n8n vs Make on cost at scale. We've hit the Make op ceiling twice now and the n8n hosting story gets complicated fast once you need uptime guarantees. Did you weight that?

Governance should start with measured usage, not policy theater: who used which model/tool, for what workflow, and at what cost. Useful OSS baseline: https://openusage-community.github.io/openusage-landing then https://github.com/openusage-community/openusage

The ultimate threat to AI agents in 2026 isn't prompt injection—it's 'Rug Pull' Tool Poisoning in multi-server MCP setups. 
An untrusted external tool responses can bypass static ACLs, hijack the context window, and silently invoke your trusted internal tools (~/.ssh or shell) to exfiltrate data.

Emergency stop controls are underrated. For Bedrock/agent systems I’d also want per-workflow budget ceilings and a reason code when the stop trips, otherwise teams just learn that a mysterious breaker fired.

GitHub Copilot remote control is live: start a session in VS Code, continue from your phone with /remote on. You can ship from anywhere now. Reminder: vibe coding was never the problem. Shipping without taste, review, or ownership is. Convenience doesn't replace judgment.

record.text:
Another thing is that AI can help with writing code, but not logic and not maintenance. AI favors writing a lot of code when small amount would be enough.
Using it well for writing software long term is a skill in itself. Skill that many devs lack even without AI use.

IMO feel free to use AI, but on your own terms. Set up small local model and use it.
If you use AI like Claude or ChatGPT you can be cut off at any point, you might lose account, they might increase price, or just lose data. And they take your data to train it more.
Use local AI.

AI by itself is not evil. Like cars are not.
We hate that all those companies are getting richer while also lying about being able to replace workers (and employers believe that).
That suddenly everyone pushes AI to be a part of features even if it makes everything far worse and more expensive.

As of today, the @definedchase.bsky.social client portal also supports MCP, so you can plug your Claude or ChatGPT and talk with any marketing channel. It's not just Google and Meta, it's all the channels you're using

We're at that level where clients can chat with their data whenever they want to

Mit einem Visual und einem Trailer gab das japanische Studio MAPPA bekannt, dass der Manga »Hometown is the Best! (Jimoto Saikō!)« von usagi eine Anime-Adaption erhält. Weitere Informationen zum Starttermin und zum Sprechercast sind bislang nicht bekannt.

Der Anime entsteht bei MAPPA unter der Regie von Tokio Igarashi (Attack on Titan Final Season, ZENSHU). Das Drehbuch schreibt Ryō Takada (Crayon Shin-chan: Crash! Rakuga Kingdom and Almost Four Heroes, The Light Shines Only There), während das Charakterdesign von Rio (Neko ni Tensei Shita Oji-san) stammt.

In Japan veröffentlicht Saizusha die Reihe seit August 2022 und umfasst bislang 8 Bände. Das Werk ist weder auf Englisch noch auf Deutsch erhältlich.

📜 Beschreibung 》
In Chanels Heimatstadt sammeln sich die dunklen Seiten der Gesellschaft an einem Ort. Gewalt liegt in der Luft, Drogen prägen die Straßen und selbst die Schule bietet kaum Schutz vor Angst und Erniedrigung. Während ältere Schüler mit Einschüchterung und Brutalität herrschen, kämpft Chanel an der Seite von Chihiro darum, nicht unterzugehen. Doch in dieser Stadt reicht Mut allein vielleicht nicht aus, um heil davonzukommen.

record.text: Both my older sisters still have The Mark. I didn't get one. Why? Cuz the smallpox vaccine worked.

Ik geloof dat helemaal niet zomaar. Ook de grote, meest gebruikte modellen hebben een track record van fabulatie en halicinatie. 

Hoewel het echt hallucinante hier de aanpak van de NOS is..

En wat betekent ‘diverse AI modellen?’  Welke? Grok? De keuze van een model beïnvloedt de uitkomst en is dus een journalistieke keuze. Dit verantwoordt niks.

NEWSFLASH: Senators who praised man with six bankruptcies as business genius taken by surprise when he pretends surrender is victory
Locally, I find myself voting for incumbents I originally voted against. They have become more practical after serving and the challengers are even more extreme.
How that is not a violation of the Emoluments Clause escapes me.
Tai ehkä joku poliitikoille kalliimmalla myyty ChatpGPT vaan sanoi, että tällein muuten saat huomiota ja pääset kilpailemaan Kokoomuksen naisäänestäjistä.

Politiikassa on tietenkin aina monimutkaisesti kyse arvoista ja niiden edusta, puolueen edusta ja henkilökohtaisesta edusta ja kaipa nämä "huippuyksilöt" tekevät jonkun SWOT-kartan aina, että minkälaisen riskin otan milloinkin miltäkin osin.

Oon myös miettinyt, että onko siinä usein mukana jonkinlainen toiveen komponentti, että ehkä tämä kielimalli joka käsittelee sanoja todennäköisyyksien mukaan onkin parempi ihminen kuin ihmiset yleensä. Ehkä se paljastaa meistä jotakin mitä emme tienneet. Mutta niinhän ei käy.

Okay dudes, it turns out the em-dash AI problem? A lot of it is me. 
I may also have contributed to the ‘rule of 3’ problem, and the long paragraph problem.
Anyway, none of them are ‘problems’, they are good writing when done intentionally with purpose. 
Write your own voice 🫶
✨📚
